content.assessment: This proposal preserves the three-track legal structure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01) entirely and works within the existing centrally administered exam mechanism (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02), modifying weighting and outreach rather than institutional architecture. From an institutional-fit standpoint this is a low-disruption intervention, but it leaves untouched the exact governance layer that current evidence identifies as the segregation engine: free school choice as a non-negotiable statutory right (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03), and the family-capital-driven coaching investment pattern documented for exam preparation (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05). Because admission remains competitive and centrally standardized rather than restructured, the proposal risks being absorbed by the same institutional logic that has historically produced stratified parental-education composition (F-live-hungarian_education_system-04) and EU-topping segregation outcomes (F-live-hungarian_education_system-06).
opportunities: Could be layered onto the existing ministerial review agenda already opened by the current minister regarding the entrance-exam system (F-live-hungarian_education_system-10)
Centralized, nationwide grading infrastructure (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02) offers an existing administrative channel for embedding SES-aware adjustments without new bureaucratic creation
strengths: Retains the nationally uniform, centrally graded exam infrastructure already in place (F-live-hungarian_education_system-02), minimizing administrative rebuild costs
Works within statutory free-choice guarantees rather than confronting them, avoiding a direct legal clash with the Act on Public Education (F-live-hungarian_education_system-03)
threats: Leaves feeder-primary-school creaming dynamics documented in the Encs case unaddressed, since track structure itself is unchanged (F-live-hungarian_education_system-07)

Ministerial characterization of 6/8-grade advantage as family-background-driven self-selection rather than value-added learning (F-live-hungarian_education_system-08) suggests that adjusting admission weighting alone may not shift the institutional dynamic the minister has already diagnosed as structural
weaknesses: Does not alter the track structure identified as institutionally entrenched since the early 1990s (F-live-hungarian_education_system-01), so the underlying selection point remains
Coaching-return dynamics documented in exam-prep investment data (F-live-hungarian_education_system-05) may simply redirect rather than eliminate family-capital advantages within the same exam format
